stack.authjs: @auth/core >=0.37 (PEER dependency, consumer-resolved; the customFetch named export exists only from 0.37.0 — npm latest dist-tag lags at 0.34.3; next-auth v5 re-exports customFetch)
stack.dpop: @jeswr/solid-dpop (git: jeswr/solid-dpop#main — RFC 9449 proofs; bundled into dist)
stack.flow: Solid-OIDC authorization-code + PKCE (S256) + state + nonce + DPoP, via the Auth.js customFetch seam
stack.modules: ESM (committed self-contained dist/ for github: installs)
stack.test: vitest ^4.x (faithful Map-backed mock OP — real ES256 DPoP-proof verification + §8 nonce, no live IdP)

federation.membershipTodo: SDK (not an app). `Solid(config)` returns an Auth.js `OIDCConfig<SolidProfile>` for next-auth v5 / @auth/core: Solid-OIDC discovery + PKCE(S256) + state + nonce + DPoP-bound (sender-constrained) tokens, injecting the RFC 9449 DPoP proof on the token endpoint via the Auth.js `customFetch` seam (Auth.js does not do DPoP itself). Composes @jeswr/solid-dpop for the proofs (ES256, asymmetric-only) — reimplements neither DPoP nor OIDC; Auth.js owns the OAuth orchestration (deliberately NOT @jeswr/solid-openid-client, which would fight Auth.js's own flow). Verified-WebID-only (fail-closed). Also exports `solidDpopFetch` for DPoP-attaching pod requests from a persisted session + the jwt/session persistence helpers. It is a login SDK, not a federation member; it holds no federation membership of its own.
